25 January 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Cuba's president observes drills, vows high cost for any US aggression Press TV 25 Jan 2026 -- Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel on Saturday oversaw extensive military exercises, saying the drills are aimed at deterring potential aggression from the United States and making any attack "too costly for imperialism to contemplate."

  • U.S. Africa Command cross-functional team visits Democratic Republic of the Congo AFRICOM 25 Jan 2026 -- A cross-functional team from U.S. Africa Command (AFRICOM) traveled to the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) Jan. 20-23 to meet with leaders from the Armed Forces of the DRC (FARDC) and explore ways to enhance security cooperation efforts.

  • Russia Does Not Rule Out Non-Strategic Nuclear Weapon Drills With Belarus - Foreign Ministry Sputnik 25 Jan 2026 -- Russia does not rule out holding exercises with Belarus to test the use of non-strategic nuclear weapons in the future, Alexey Polishchuk, director of the Second CIS Department at the Russian Foreign Ministry, told Sputnik.

  • China resolute in winning anti-corruption war in military: PLA Daily Global Times 25 Jan 2026 -- An editorial to be published by a flagship newspaper of the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) on Sunday vows to fight and win the war against corruption in the military.

  • FIRST TRAINING SQUADRON OF INDIAN NAVY STRENGTHENS MARITIME BONDS AT INDONESIA India PIB 25 Jan 2026 -- Boosting maritime engagement with Indonesia, a member nation of the Indian Ocean Naval Symposium (IONS), and advancing the vision of MAHASAGAR, the Indian Navy's First Training Squadron (1TS) departed Belawan on 23 Jan 2026.

  • Remarks by PM Netanyahu at the Government Meeting Following Approval of the NIS 6.2 Billion 2026 Reservists Assistance and Appreciation Plan Israel - Prime Minister 25 Jan 2026

  • PNP steps up coordination with AFP in Mindanao to deter terrorism Philippine News Agency 25 Jan 2026 -- Philippine National Police (PNP) acting chief Lt. Gen. Jose Melencio Nartatez Jr. has ordered strict monitoring and intensified intelligence-gathering to help deter terrorism-related recruitment and training in Mindanao.

  • European Politicians Incapable of Resisting Trump's Assertiveness - Kremlin Sputnik 25 Jan 2026 -- The current generation of European politicians is unable to counter US President Donald Trump's assertiveness, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Sunday.

  • Joint Statement on the Third U.S.-Oman Strategic Dialogue US Dept. of State 25 Jan 2026 -- The text of the following statement was released by the Governments of the United States of America and the Sultanate of Oman on the occasion of the Third U.S.-Oman Strategic Dialogue.
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Iraqi Prime Minister Mohammed Shiaa al-Sudani US Dept. of State 25 Jan 2026

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Prime Minister's Office Announcement Israel - Prime Minister 25 Jan 2026 -- Since the weekend, IDF forces have been conducting a wide-scale operation to locate the fallen hostage, Master-Sgt. Ran Gvili, of blessed memory.
  • PM Netanyahu, Minister of Construction and Housing Haim Katz and Settlements Minister Orit Strock announced at the Government Meeting the Establishment of Five New Communities in the Negev Israel - Prime Minister 25 Jan 2026 -- Prime Minister Netanyahu proposed that one of the communities be named "Renanim," in memory of the last hostage, and hero of Israel, the late Ran Gvili (of blessed memory)

  • Dismantling of an Approximately Four-Kilometer Underground Tunnel Route and Locating Rocket Launchers Israel Defense Forces 25 Jan 2026

  • Two civilians Killed, multiple injured in Israeli attacks across Gaza amid relentless truce violations Press TV 25 Jan 2026 -- The latest Israeli attacks have killed at least two civilians and injured several others across the besieged Gaza Strip amid persistent violations of the ceasefire agreement.

  • Israeli warplanes conduct new aggressions across southern Lebanon Press TV 25 Jan 2026 -- Israeli warplanes have conducted a new wave of aggression across southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, killing at least two people and injuring others, Lebanese authorities reported.
  • Lebanon failing prisoners held by Israel, Hezbollah chief says Press TV 25 Jan 2026 -- The Secretary-General of the Lebanese resistance movement Hezbollah has criticized the Lebanese government for failing to secure the release of prisoners held by Israel.

  • Russia Can Send $1Bln From Frozen Assets to Peace Board If US Banks Enact Order - Official Sputnik 25 Jan 2026 -- The process for potentially transferring $1 billion from Russia's frozen assets to the Board of Peace is quite simple: US banks need to execute Moscow's payment order, Deputy Chief of Staff of the Russian Presidential Executive Office Maxim Oreshkin said on Sunday.

  • MSF to disclose limited list for Gaza staff after Israel revoked its aid licenses Press TV 25 Jan 2026 -- After Israel revoked the operating licenses of Doctors Without Borders (MSF), the medical charity says it will share a limited staff list to continue aid operations across Gaza and other Israeli-occupied territories.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Navy continues search, rescue ops for sunken cargo ship in WPS Philippine News Agency 25 Jan 2026 -- The Philippine Navy on Sunday said it is continuing search and rescue (SAR) operations in the West Philippine Sea following the sinking of a Singaporean-flagged cargo vessel carrying Filipino crew members.

  • Taliban Cracks Down On Private Children's Homes In Afghanistan RFE/RL 25 Jan 2026 -- The Taliban has launched a crackdown on private children's homes that provide shelter, education, and care to thousands across Afghanistan.

  • Yemen's Ansarullah warns of US, Israel soft war on West Asia nations Press TV 25 Jan 2026 -- A high-ranking official from Yemen's Ansarullah resistance movement has cautioned against psychological operations of the United States and Israel in West Asia, stressing that they attempt to shape public opinion before any act of aggression against regional nations.

  • On 25 January 2026, security forces conducted an intelligence based operation in Panjgur District of Balochistan, on reported presence of terrorists belonging to Indian proxy, Fitna al Hindustan. ISPR Pakistan 25 Jan 2026

News Reports

  • Democrats threaten govt. shutdown after second ICE killing in Minneapolis Press TV 25 Jan 2026 -- The chances of a partial US government shutdown have gone up after a second fatality at the hands of federal immigration agents in Minneapolis, Minnesota.
  • Minneapolis shooting witnesses, victim's family reject US administration's 'lies' about killing Press TV 25 Jan 2026 -- Eyewitnesses to a recent fatal shooting in the US city of Minneapolis and the family of the victim have challenged the federal account of the incident, saying the government is spreading "lies" about what happened.
  • US Homeland Security Secretary Accuses Minnesota Governor of Provoking Protesters Sputnik 25 Jan 2026 -- US Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem accused Minnesota Governor Tim Walz of provoking protesters, claiming that the man, who was shot and killed of Friday, was armed and dangerous.
  • Third fatal officer-involved shooting in Minneapolis within a month exposes enforcement flaws, may heighten federal-state tension: expert Global Times 25 Jan 2026 -- Authorities confirmed US local time Saturday that the man shot dead by US federal agents earlier in the morning in Minneapolis, Minnesota was a US citizen, marking a third shooting involving federal law enforcement officers in the city this month, the Xinhua News Agency reported on Sunday morning.
  • 'How many more have to die?': Minneapolis mayor demands Trump end 'invasion' Press TV 25 Jan 2026 -- Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has sharply criticized the presence of federal law enforcement in the city following the fatal shooting of a 37-year-old man by federal agents, demanding an end to the operation and calling on President Donald Trump to remove federal officers from Minneapolis.

  • Visa Restrictions for Transitional Presidential Council (TPC) Members for Supporting Gangs US Dept. of State 25 Jan 2026 -- The Department of State is taking steps to impose visa restrictions and revoke the visas of two (2) Transitional Presidential Council (TPC) members and their immediate family members (spouse and children).

  • Chairman Crawford on the Passing of AHI Victim Mike Beck US House of Representatives Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence 25 Jan 2026 -- Today, House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence Chairman Rick Crawford (AR-01) issued the following statement on the passing of Mike Beck, a counterintelligence officer who was the victim of an Anomalous Health Incident (AHI) in 1996.

  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 25 Jan 2026

  • Iran's Internet Blackout Persists As Report Says Protest Death Toll May Exceed 30,000 RFE/RL 25 Jan 2026 -- Iran's nationwide Internet blackout remained largely in place as the reported death toll from recent protests continued to rise, with one account saying the number of fatalities may exceed 30,000.

  • Kata'ib Hezbollah calls on resistance fighters to prepare for 'war' in support of Iran Press TV 25 Jan 2026 -- Iraq's Kata'ib Hezbollah (Hezbollah Brigades) movement has voiced its readiness to enter any possible war in defense of Iran, calling on resistance fighters worldwide to mobilize for this purpose.

  • Prime Minister Netanyahu and Minister Regev meet with Patriots delegation Israel - Prime Minister 25 Jan 2026 -- Prime Minister Netanyahu and Minister Miri Regev met today, at the Prime Minister's Office, with the Patriots delegation - parliament members from Hungary, Austria, Spain and France.

  • The Presidents of Lithuania and Poland discussed changes in Europe's security environment President of the Republic of Lithuania 25 Jan 2026 -- On Sunday, President Gitanas Nausėda met in Vilnius with Polish President Karol Nawrocki, who had arrived for the commemoration of the 1863-1864 Uprising.

  • Farmers In Uzbekistan Say Land Forcibly Taken For 'Chinese Projects' RFE/RL 25 Jan 2026 -- Tensions are rising again in Uzbekistan's eastern Andijon region, where farmers say they are being coerced into "voluntarily" surrendering their land to local authorities -- plots of farmland that in some cases are later leased to Chinese investors.
